name: SimECG-N
description: SimECG-N is a synthetic 1-D ECG corpus generated in this repository with a physiologically inspired simulator. The dataset contains only signal arrays (single-lead, 10-second windows) and no identifiable patient-derived metadata.

distribution.name: simecg_n_manifest
distribution.description: Per-file manifest for the SimECG-N corpus subset files.

distribution.name: simecg_n_train_split
distribution.description: Generated train split generated from one run of the SimECG-N pipeline.

distribution.name: simecg_n_val_split
distribution.description: Generated validation split generated from one run of the SimECG-N pipeline.

recordSet.name: simecg_n_file_records
recordSet.description: One record per stored waveform file.

fields.name: file_path
fields.description: Relative location of each file inside the dataset archive.

fields.name: seed
fields.description: Random seed used for synthetic generation.

fields.name: syn_mode
fields.description: Scenario mode used by the generator (pt or clf).

fields.name: record_count_expected
fields.description: Expected number of waveforms in this file.

fields.name: record_count_actual
fields.description: Observed number of waveforms after generation; TODO before final release.

fields.name: sampling_rate_hz
fields.description: Sampling frequency used when generating each waveform.

fields.name: n_leads
fields.description: Number of signal channels per waveform.

intendedUse: Pretraining and benchmarking ECG SSL models.
Method comparison for generative ECG signal pipelines.
nonRecommendedUse: Clinical decision support or diagnosis without independent validation.
Substitution for real raw ECG waveforms in regulated care settings.
knownLimitations: Synthetic signals are generated from parametric priors and augmentations, not measured physiology.
Generator outputs may underrepresent rare morphologies and demographic subgroup differences.
Temporal dynamics may drift relative to specific hospital/device acquisition settings.
biases: Potential over-representation of normal-to-mild-abnormal morphology in default settings.
Limited evaluation coverage for uncommon rhythm patterns and artifact-heavy recordings.

sensitiveInformation.deidentificationMethod: None required: signals are fully synthetic and no subject metadata is stored.

socialImpact: Supports safe synthetic-data experimentation and avoids redistributing raw ECG records.
Not intended to alter clinical workflow directly; use strictly for non-clinical research.
provenance: Generated by src/prep/SimECG-N/gen_ecg.py using repo-wide settings from config.yaml.
Parameter definitions are loaded from src/prep/SimECG-N/resources/*.yaml.
Amplitude perturbations, beat perturbations, and augmentation are controlled by NumPy RNG and seed.
reproducibility: Re-run with the same config, seed, and syncfg for deterministic generation (subject to timeout behavior).
Record manifest and config snapshots together with experiment logs.
